title: Tomdoc Cheat Sheet
description: A guide to writing clear and consistent documentation using Tomdoc.

commands.command:
# Public: Brief description of the method
#
# args - Description of arguments
#
# Returns the result of the method
commands.description: Define a public method with description
commands.scenario: Define a public method with description

commands.command:
# Internal: Brief description of the method
#
# args - Description of arguments
#
# Returns the result of the method
commands.description: Define a private method with description
commands.scenario: Define a private method with description

commands.command:
# Public: Brief description of the class
#
# Purpose: Explain the purpose of the class
#
# Examples
#
#   Example usage of the class

# Arguments
#
# arg1 - Description of the first argument
# arg2 - Description of the second argument

# Returns
#
# Description of what the method returns
commands.description: Use 'Returns' tag to describe return values
commands.scenario: Use 'Returns' tag to describe return values

commands.command:
# Raises
#
# ExceptionName - Description of the exception raised

# Examples
#
#   Example usage of the method
#   method_name(arg1, arg2)
commands.description: Provide an example of method usage
commands.scenario: Provide an example of method usage

commands.command:
# Examples
#
#   Example usage of the class
#   class_instance = ClassName.new

# Public: Initializes a new instance of the class
#
# args - Initialization parameters
#
# Returns a new instance of the class

# Public: Brief description of the method
#
# Returns the result of the method
commands.description: Document a method with no arguments
commands.scenario: Document a method with no arguments

commands.command:
# Public: Brief description of the method
#
# Returns
#
# String - Description if condition A
# Integer - Description if condition B
commands.description: Document a method with multiple return types
commands.scenario: Document a method with multiple return types
